I'm shedding all this garbage trauma of:
1) hiding artistic expression bc art came off as pointless, silly, and dumb to engineering bros I lived with 
2) making myself small & hiding skills and capabilities after it triggered insecure past partners who would then put me down

As I'm doing this, I realize you can be all of these things:
Artistic, technically adept, feminine, leading visions for projects, bringing dreams to life, ambitious, opinionated, graceful, kind, collaborative, nurturing, socially adept, leading in your own way, still "dateable"

Stupid ideas injected into me that I'm deconstructing and erasing:
1) "you're good at art and writing because you didn't grow up being as social as me" - ok, decade-old news. now pls tell me where I'm socially awkward. At all. Please 😌

2) "artistic/poetic ppl tend to be awk & lack external world skills" - Dang why do i feel comfortable & get along w anyone in any envrnmt, then?

3) "ppl engage w u bc they think you're attractive" - maybe initially & even then only some ppl; ppl *stay* engaged bc of other depths

4) *more bs pointing to achievements tied to looks* - simply the stupidest, most reductive way to look at someone

If you're always doing your best and put your heart into what you do, treat ppl well, etc.. cute is a plus. What do you want me to do about it, wear a mask??

5) "you have a lot of masculine energy/you're not in your feminine/you're not nurturing/who could ever date you" - for demonstrating understanding, having ideas, being able to hold discussions, all while being "yes, and" instead of debating.. fr?? Seriously? So.. stop thinking?

I know this thread comes off as particularly vulnerable and oversharey but holy sh*t did I make myself smaaalllll and quiet and even completely silly for some time, afraid to demonstrate intelligence or competency bc of other peoples' insecurity, anger, jealousy, meanness

I hope reading this helps someone who's experienced similar realize how you don't have to stop pursuing your goals, expressing opinions, or standing up for yourself bc you buy into things insecure people say to bring you down. I did, for a little while. I'm still recovering a lil

Some ppl w insecurities will work on theirs (insecurities are natural & ok to have) but still be supportive of/happy for you!
Other times it's "nice until you surpass" & then they're triggered, & they either course correct to be supportive, else...yikes. You gotta get outta there

And some are just ppl who put you down from the start if you're shining at all 

Don't get gaslit by comments about your gender, appearance, hobbies and tastes, etc. Don't let others undermine your passion, self-trust, and motivation. Stay shining and doing what you love. Pls 🥹

The ideal MO: Being and creating. By all means, play in life, but refuse to play games

The game you're playing where it feels like play and fun is different than the act of "playing games"

Games are created:
- in any kind of competition
- in any chase for external validation
- internalizing standards that you didn't set yourself
- if anyone else's voice is even slightly in your head
- when self-trust is weak or undermined

I'm still working through this freedom of play myself. Creative projects are typically play for me & I noticed a lot of voices that weren't mine popping up this week in the process of sharing ideas w collaborators: fears that they/I wouldn't be received well (tho they were), etc.

yesterday i tweeted about some of the voices i was hearing and it felt cathartic to do so, and it was so lovely to see a supportive community response and it felt like a grounding reality check 🥹 thank you all, sincerely

obviously the most powerful voice to develop is the one within, but i think it's a beautiful aspect of community to be able to be supportive and help develop and strengthen the inner voice too, so i am deeply appreciative of this space to process, share, and be affirmed <3

@QiaochuYuan @appleMechanic yeah it was an ex & it was said during conflict (as most things are). for balance when we ended he said "you're a catch & you'll find someone" too. he's not the focus anymore but the ripple effect of repeat patterns in a relationship & impact of one-liners can still be wild haha
i'm aware & accountable that what gives "other voices" power ultimately is insecurities (either old, or created) within me that need resolution. this in and of itself is good data!

for anything related to sharing creative work i've adopted a "the only way is through" approach - this can apply to many uncomfortable things in general. do it anyway, until you see what you can do & you rebuild your self-trust. watch other voices fade; yours is once again center

to other voices that contribute to self-doubt i can literally be like "shh. i am not playing your game. i got sh*t to do"

if it's my own voice that's contributing to self-doubt that's some self-love work which is always nice :D & oddly, comes more naturally to me than the above

storytelling is powerful for personal and collective peace if the narrative is "all beings moving towards universal love." if you hold onto stories filled with resentment it may provide short-term reprieve via distancing for self-preservation, but inhibits perceiving the nondual

similarly, if you hold on to stories that ruminate over your/others' mistakes, it doesn't allow for separation from the past into the present. it doesn't allow the space needed to perceive that any being in the present can exist as anything independent of how it was in the past

reflection is how we grow. reflect & grow from the past, but let go of the story that insists "because A happened, it will always be B." it's detrimental when a story has the past clouding the infinite potential of the present moment

process and integrate pain & be mindful of using it as a "distancing crutch" because it fractures the interconnectedness of all beings. ultimately, peace is returning to this view that we are all dynamic, constantly evolving manifestations of one existence in this fabric of life

through mindfulness you catch yourself in stories that have you straying from this view. let yourself and others grow, and when it's time, let go of narratives that reinforce distancing, separation, and rigid identification with the past. write instead the story of unity and love

Idk who needs to hear this, but as soon as you accept that there are absolutely people who will be unkind to you or treat you poorly despite you being nothing but kind to them, and that is just how the world is sometimes, you'll find peace more easily

It's not your job to keep giving and giving that kindness and hold out to see if they'll return it at some point some day. Some never will. The same goes for apologies. If you're someone who is highly accountable & apologizes, that doesn't mean others who hurt you ever will

If you have high conscientiousness & integrity, you might project your understanding & believe others you interact with would have the same - but a fact of life is how that's highly unlikely to apply to everyone you know.  You have integrity in full. Others straight-up may not
Take your beautiful traits in stride & go find those who match your level of conscientiousness, integrity, empathy, & accountability. They exist

But never hold out for ppl who don't match that to do or say something that will "make things right" because they may never. You make it right for yourself when *you* up & go where you're loved. Because you are worthy of pure, honest, integrous love when you yourself embody it 🤍

In the whole "forgiveness is for you, not the other person" & "closure is something you give yourself" paradigm I'm working to fully let go of wishing for apologies that will never happen. I feel like I almost have but it hasn't been easy. The road's paved with sadness & anger

Forgiveness is a LOT easier when people sincerely apologize but some never do and never will. Some will never be sorry in the first place. So yeah, it becomes nearly all on me and I can do it. I am doing it. I'm almost where I want to be with it

I had an experience where I wondered if someone from my past would be able to fully acknowledge some painful things bc time had passed & they presented outwardly as "loving and awakened". Upon interaction, I initially found myself hoping for this but realized my delulu-ness later

Bc it didn't happen. Not even close. And it was maybe the final karmic thread to cut. No I don't "need" the apologies. Would it have been nice? Yeah. Will it ever happen? No, and I accept that. It's peace for me to understand I cannot expect everyone to embody standards I have

Dear lil Nadia, mom will never admit to doing those things and apologize. Your ex partners, ex friends, and former roommates won't either 

Your peace shall be your peace, independent of anyone. Take your ability to love and care the way you do and be among those who have it too
Side note: are ppl actually that awakened if they can't say a sincere "I'm so sorry I put you through that" when "that" comes up in conversation? Lol

Beware of the spiritual ego that identifies with goodness & purity so much that it de-identifies with the part that is capable of deep hurt and wrongdoing. Refusal to see it or admit to it keeps this part in a deep, suppressed, shameful, rejected state. It's avoidance and bypass

You're in a continuous state of self-rejection. "That's not me. That was me, but not anymore. It's not me now" and it leads to cognitive dissonance

Vs. "I fked up. I've made mistakes. I see this part of myself. I own it. Let me understand you. Let's truly see how we do better"

It's literally okay to make mistakes. You absolutely will. Have you truly owned them, though?
Or are you externalizing blame (everyone else/everything else was the problem) or bypassing ("I accept that I wasn't ideal but that's not me now") as you shove your shadow into a closet?

Inner child work & shadow integration are the yin/yang of the inner parent

Inner child work: Seeing and loving that part, creating safety for that part to talk to you, increasing self-compassion cushions

Shadow integration: Less coddling. You rly negotiate w/ your "dark" parts

Theory: If you're not integrating your shadow then you're going to just coddle that inner child and suck at personal accountability or accountability to others. And if you're not doing inner child work, shadow integration can feel like rapture w/o a self-love cushion to fall on

Sometimes you can integrate shadow by doing inner child work & sometimes you can do inner child work when you do shadow integration - just like there's a little bit of yang in yin & yin in yang

And even if you don't know wtf either of these are you probs do it if you're healing

Inner yang/masc is there to be like "yo, cut your own shit and fragility, let's rly look at your dark side in the mirror & figure this out. you GOT this" and inner yin/feminine is there to be like "damn yeah that was kinda tough, here's hugs, love. love you & you're doing great"

My friends joke that I'm oblivious to ppl's crushes and interest but I'm absolutely aware. I just carry on like I don't notice it (if I'm not interested back). It's a skill to not let other peoples' energy impact how you would otherwise be operating

Your energetic boundaries have to be strong when you feel other ppl's energy *on* you & you still want to be authentically yourself. If you let it break into your energetic space without your wanting to or change your behavior, you're in people-pleasing/self-compromising mode

It's taken a lot of heavy mental-lifting in the past. I'd feel so sticky icky contaminated from certain attention after leaving a place sometimes, but now I feel like I'm in the right crowds for me, or unreciprocated energy otherwise rolls off like water on a duck's feathers

And for the record it's okay to have crushes! It's okay to have interest and express it too. Just wanted to highlight that yes ppl are aware even if they don't seem like it. Like no we're not dumb. Do you know how much energetic protection it takes to *stay* warm & approachable?

if you have any interest in tantra/tantric yoga, be hardcore about having right-hand path/white tantra/sattvic basics down first before you immerse in communities. there's a mix of white/red/black tantra practitioners in communities & on the surface it will look the same at first

left-hand (red/black) path is not inherently dangerous but it has risks. in fact, ppl will often use a mix of paths. deity yoga and rituals can be powerful. "darker" deities esp can help move and process a lot and give a lot of personal power. the problem, however, IS power

you won't hear about this power on twitter/the internet. i couldn't talk about it without the layman saying "lol that's bs". if you go deep into it you *will* encounter occult practice. you NEED to know how to return to your bodymind & listen to your intuition

by that i mean have your sattvic basics down such that you can return to that which is directly observable in your bodymind (step away from deities, other ppl, ritual for a sec) & return to your own voice. otherwise, you can & will be influenced by ritual, leaders, outer voices

if you're not careful, you can get entangled with ppl who practice for the pursuit of power, or you yourself may start to pursue power. that's the value behind the "may i practice out of love for myself, w a desire to know the Truth, for the benefit of all beings" intention-set

i was more vocal about tantra before but now i'm cautious about advocating for it w/o warning when it comes to the broader community. develop & listen to your intuition & you'll know which things you encounter are a "NO" & you can still keep all other beautiful teachings w you

also the occult isn't inherently bad - white magic, full moon ceremonies, etc have made it into modern witch culture & are innocuous & cathartic! but there are some (imo *unncessary* - ppl absolutely do it for power) "dark" practices i won't get into. these are "YO beware"

@danielbrottman referring to classical tantra here!
white - focus on purity of mind & body. deals with mindfulness, meditation, etc. upper chakras
red - deals w the entire embodied experience. *can* include sexual practice and taboo stuff but it doesn't have to. all chakras
black - siddhi stuff

@danielbrottman a lot of tantra that's focused on integration before transcendence will still draw on concepts in red tantra and is a mix of red/white

red/black or black mix is where it gets into the manipulation and control risks that come with siddhis (special powers) & occult practice

@danielbrottman and one doesn't need siddhis for the spiritual path and might attain them but they're not the goal. it's like if you can see and control certain things, should you start to use it for your own power/gain or let it be and move forward in purity. black can rly focus on the former

@danielbrottman they are separate but if you're going into the embodied experience and working on integrating shame around sexuality you touch on red concepts that are helpful but you aren't steeped in red practices

boundaries get fuzzy when *communities* have ppl practicing various combos

Those "often imitated, never replicated" ppl - the ones who make what ppl try to accomplish look easy? They do what they do out of pure love: for the craft & by extension themselves, and for others participating. They're not trying to sell you anything; it comes naturally to them
Authenticity never lies and other people can feel the difference

These people do not need anything from you and therefore are in an open, freely sharing state. They don't need you to validate their worth, intelligence, beauty, coolness, skill, creations. They don't need you to buy a single thing, whether it's a perception or service

And that's the difference you sense when someone does something skilled and with perceptible ease - they're not performing, they're playing. They're not pleading to be seen, but they are visible. They're not seeking to take, they are sharing.

6. Asked a monk at my temple how i reconcile liberation w wanting marriage & family. He responded "Go for those things. You'll want it, you'll get it, and then at some point when you're old, marriage is living like brother & sister. That's when one can rly go for liberation"

(a lil weird for an analogy, i know - but he means lust, strong desire, couple goals die down and you can focus on Spirit in this later period)

@minnowpark 7. "You can be kind to people but you're just going to have to accept that some people are never going to be kind to you no matter how you've treated them. It might sound harsh but it's just something to accept about the world"

My first therapist ever in 2017. This helped a lot

8. "She's a big girl, she'll tell us if she needs something"

This was back in 2020. A friend said this to me when i worried about our other friend on a night out. I learned to chill out & trust other adults to communicate needs to me in some situations & not be hypervigilant

9. "Femininity can help in the workplace sometimes"

A feminine friend said this. I got so used to hearing "you get this treatment bc you're a girl" but SO what??? Wow ppl are inclined to interact w you positively? And? Where's the crime? This was an acceptance/aha moment

10. "If he doesn't know what he wants, he's a waste of time"

MANY male friends of mine giving me blunt dating advice and bringing me to my senses over the past year. Men know what they want and if he's unsure or uncertain I learned it's a good bye (& say bye sooner than later)

i went through a phase of getting irked over misuse and misappropriation of the words "spiritual" or "divine" by people, "spirituality" and love platitudes used as persona/marketing strategies by grifter-esque people financially preying on their hurting and vulnerable peers, and…

i went through a phase of getting irked over misuse and misappropriation of the words "spiritual" or "divine" by people, "spirituality" and love platitudes used as persona/marketing strategies by grifter-esque people financially preying on their hurting and vulnerable peers, and people speaking like they are the second coming of christ.

then i realized it (at least partially) can also be understood as a projection of what was my own dissonance and lack of inner peace. while there are absolutely people taking meretricious advantage of these concepts, others do mean well and are helpful. we ultimately do learn from each other - both directly and indirectly - whether it's via the repetitive nature of hearing Mark Manson or the Buddha regurgitated for the 11th time of "guy who reads books n blogs", or through original thought, or through actual, embodied wisdom, we can look back and say "yeah that was foolish" or "wow, that was helpful!" and learn either way

people learn in the way they learn. we're all trying our best out here. we all have grappled at times for importance, purpose, meaning, peace, and healing in this human experience. sometimes it comes off as performative, other times it is authentic, and we uncover growth edges in the ways that we do. sometimes we're overconfident and other times there is warranted wisdom. i see it all more and more clearly over time, but it would be silly of me to assert that my vision is crystal clear at this age. i too, am a child of the universe.

while i am wary and aware of grifters and inauthentic people (and i encourage you to be aware of this too), sometimes i also find peace in understanding that it's not the blind leading the blind, but rather a lot of squinting and earnest people learning to discern shapes in near and far and seeing better together over time. you and i are squinters until we are not.

there's this golden window where someone desires your apology before their mind switches to "i have lost respect for you so what's an apology from you going to mean to me, anyway?" this is the coping, human nature of grief and rage as one goes through it

and when it all passes they ultimately find forgiveness but you have also become absolutely irrelevant. the iron gates have closed (read: boundaries are up) and you're no longer welcome on palace grounds; you've gone from esteemed guest to simple outsider because they've long…

and when it all passes they ultimately find forgiveness but you have also become absolutely irrelevant. the iron gates have closed (read: boundaries are up) and you're no longer welcome on palace grounds; you've gone from esteemed guest to simple outsider because they've long burned your invitation in all the time that you did not accept it. it's a calm "wishing you well" through the palace gates

so go apologize where apologies are due and expedite their healing, or just wait long enough until your relevance dies to them. the ending is the same; it's the rate of healing and your reputation in their eyes that will differ

If you feel lonely in your spiritual beliefs based on tweets and interactions you see out and about ("I'll never find a partner who has the same vibes as me!! :( it's so rare"), I'll let you in on a little secret: There are a lot of peaceful, spiritually examined people who are…

If you feel lonely in your spiritual beliefs based on tweets and interactions you see out and about ("I'll never find a partner who has the same vibes as me!! :( it's so rare"), I'll let you in on a little secret: There are a lot of peaceful, spiritually examined people who are just living their life and they don't talk about anything but spirituality out loud, even though they have a deeply examined existence and practice.

I remember when I graduated high school and started college, a lot of guys went from skinny to newbie lifters and "do u even lift bro" was a wholeass meme. Guys new to lifting who just started seeing some gains couldn't stop talking about lifting and their workouts, gains, workout routines, etc. They helped each other learn and improve in the gym but there was also this element of "I'm new to this and this makes me feel good!! I need people to know!" And guess what, that's okay. :P Fast-forward 10 years and these same guys my age are jacked but they're not constantly talking about lifting. They can just take it for granted. They work out every day but that broadcasting phase passed. Many just live their life with lifting being something they do regularly. IMO spirituality can be like this too; it's just not visible to the eyes.

Some may choose to be vocal about their spiritual beliefs because it helps with learning and finding community, and it helps others and that's all awesome! I've learned so much from people sharing things, from sharing things myself, and having conversations. For others, their spiritual beliefs are so much their norm that they are simply living it, joyfully and peacefully, and have been this way for a while.

So, if you feel lonely, for your mental peace and optimism: Don't assume that people around you are simpletons/can't relate/don't understand. You may be pleasantly surprised to understand that we're all a lot more complex than we let on to others or even vocalize.

Inb4 someone quotes Lao Tzu “Those who know do not speak, those who speak do not know”:
There's truth to this, but some who know some things still do speak. Sharing is good. This is why we have books, and helpful ppl, and teachers to learn from. This is not an anti-sharing post by any means! I am pro-sharing and pro-learning, but I am also pro-graduating phases of life, pro-suspending assumptions, etc :)

I asked the bestie to rewrite one of my tweets for you 🤪

"I'll rewrite your thoughtful thread with a delightfully pessimistic spin that would make life worse:

Hey there spiritual seekers! Let me crush your hopes about finding like-minded people: Those peaceful, spiritually-aware folks you're looking for? They're actively hiding from you. They've developed such sophisticated methods of camouflage that you'll never spot them in the wild. They're probably laughing at your earnest tweets about seeking connection right now.

Remember those guys who got into lifting in college? Well here's a depressing parallel: They all eventually realized the futility of self-improvement and now silently judge anyone who shows enthusiasm about anything. The only thing worse than being the annoying newbie who won't shut up about their gains is becoming the jaded veteran who can't even remember why they started lifting in the first place.

Sure, some people are vocal about their spiritual journey, but that's just because they haven't reached the final stage of spiritual development: crushing existential dread coupled with an inability to connect with others. The truly enlightened ones are just sitting alone in their rooms, too paralyzed by cosmic insignificance to even post about it.

So if you're feeling lonely, here's some terrible advice: Assume everyone around you is actually far more spiritually advanced than you but they're deliberately excluding you from their secret society. They're all having profound metaphysical discussions without you, probably at a cafe you've never even heard of.

And before someone quotes Lao Tzu - guess what? He was just a really successful troll. Those who know do not speak because they're too busy being smug about knowing. Those who speak do not know because they haven't yet realized that everything is pointless anyway.

Remember: The path to true misery is paved with the realization that everyone else is having profound spiritual experiences while you're still here posting about wanting to find your tribe. Good luck! 🙃

(How's that for making life worse? 😈)"

The "who's owning who here" situations are hilarious. Eg. this whole thing where critics of AOC are like "ooOOoOo she's too dumb to understand sarcasm" - that's not what's happening. Earnest acceptance of words that can be taken positively if taken literally juxtapose the other party's impulse to tear you down if they meant anything but good. You find ppl flustered and going in harder on the attack while you're just like "hah what ☺️" as they let their character speak for themselves. The offense destroys themselves if they choose to, but you haven't chosen that for them

I learned unbotheredness early on when I'd get called "nerd" in elementary school for being Hermione-esque in class & I thought they were just calling me smart so I'd be like "thanks!" And kids would be like "wait no-". Ofc after age 6 I learned it was an insult but I already saw how flustered ppl get when you won't take their insult as an insult and are just like "?? :D"

 I am *for* accountability when someone who cares is trying to genuinely communicate something to help you or your relationship with them - there's a difference between unbothered and defensive/dismissive. So it's either give genuine, constructive feedback or watch it completely backfire without someone else doing anything but be a mirror to you

If you feel at peace on your own but your peace falls apart when relationally stress-tested then your framework of emotional processing and discernment is probably filled with avoidance and bypass - whether it's a spiritual framework or a la "subtle art of not giving a fk"

You…

If you feel at peace on your own but your peace falls apart when relationally stress-tested then your framework of emotional processing and discernment is probably filled with avoidance and bypass - whether it's a spiritual framework or a la "subtle art of not giving a fk"

You might use whatever framework to "make yourself feel better" and "find your peace"...when alone. But other people are mirrors to the parts of you that have evaded accountability to them.

Robust, spiritual maturity operates on a balance between equanimity, accountability, and energetic boundaries in relational contexts.

& Putting up rigid energetic boundaries and cutting people off in situations where you have things yourself to be accountable for yet haven't owned in full isn't some noble act of "protecting your peace"; it's dismissal, avoidance, and evasion of accountability

Spirit, consciousness, and woo is entering the mainstream with even Rogan talking about the Telepathy Tapes now & I hope to gosh the true essence of why all this matters isn't lost.

We'll see the rise of more charlatans appropriating this knowledge and making claims for status,…

Spirit, consciousness, and woo is entering the mainstream with even Rogan talking about the Telepathy Tapes now & I hope to gosh the true essence of why all this matters isn't lost.

We'll see the rise of more charlatans appropriating this knowledge and making claims for status, power, attention, personal gain, "specialness" - they may claim to sell you something that can only truly be found freely, deeply, personally, and naturally. The more information exists out there, the more material people have to use to inform their performances eg. "Ah, this is how I may better sound like a prophet of the people!" And this isn't all malicious. Some people really want to feel special and will delude themselves into identifying with certain powers and then promote themselves as such. They're not bad, but maybe they do wish they had something special about them and this performance will be that for them.

We'll also see people opening their minds more and becoming more curious, challenging what they believe is the fundamental nature of reality, and evolving their views into one that's more inclusive. The more information exists out there, the more material people have to be like "Ah, I am hearing more and I am understanding more, and my existential views are becoming more inclusive and loving."

Please be aware of both and know the difference! Lies and truth, principled and hypocritical, performance and authenticity - these will always and inevitably be two sides of the same coin.

If there's one thing I've learned this year, it's to really learn to attune to one's own intuition so much that you can see through anyone where it's appropriate or necessary. Don't fall for bs. Discern true intention and that which is unifying on principle from that which is just strategically applied using a personal rubric or for ego fodder

It unfortunately can add to noise when there are genuine, authentic, and verifiable people too that have completely different intentions and purpose for sharing.

We'll also see people opening their minds more and becoming more curious, challenging what they believe is the fundamental nature of reality, and evolving their views into one that's more inclusive. The more information exists out there, the more material people have to be like "Ah, I am hearing more and I am understanding more, and my existential views are becoming more inclusive and loving."

Please be aware of both and know the difference! Lies and truth, principled and hypocritical, performance and authenticity - these will always and inevitably be two sides of the same coin.

Modern jerk-ness doesn't fit that "physically hot jock that's an outward jerk or player dates the sweet cheerleader" trope. Everyone knows this trope. In this trope, she goes on to fall for her sweet, nerdy best friend that treats her better.

I didn't understand that modern…

Modern jerk-ness doesn't fit that "physically hot jock that's an outward jerk or player dates the sweet cheerleader" trope. Everyone knows this trope. In this trope, she goes on to fall for her sweet, nerdy best friend that treats her better.

I didn't understand that modern jerks aren't out here necessarily outright bullying people. It's actually a lot more subtle. I'd often have this strawman comparison standard to "well there are those hot but pure jerk guys you see on TV, he does x y z and he cares about a b c" without realizing THAT'S BASIC RESPECT. I do that. Lots of men do that. Even extremely conventionally attractive ones.

Fk that scarcity mindset that media pushes that has ppl believing you have to choose between extremely physically attractive and an actual, great personality. You really, really don't. And the modern jerks aren't exaggerated caricatures of bullies, players, etc - they're a lot more subtle. They know the nice guy front to a T. It's the man who is respectful 80% of the time but disrespectful the other 20%. And they get away with it because you don't know better.

Don't tolerate disrespect. Not 20%, not even 5%. It should be 0%. From them and from you, whether it's towards others or towards you. If it's not 0%, GTFO of there. And don't even let them make excuses for it or blame it on how you were behaving, and especially if you were nothing but respectful - if you're a kind person who nominally does take accountability you might eat it up if you let your standards or sense of self falter.

Seeing abusive people write like virtuous, loving people that are "spiritually evolved" without even being able to apologize for all they've done made me realize how twitter usage can be like beauty filters, Photoshop, and heavy makeup as part of human psyche catfishing

"Maybe if I perform like this it'll feel real and people can believe it! Maybe it'll hide what's underneath"

"Maybe if I tweet about love enough it'll bury everything I did so I never have to look at it, identify it, or admit to it. Or just until I feel better. This is me now!"

There's people on here that will sooner tweet "I love myself. I forgive myself. It's okay to be an asshole sometimes. I love everyone!!" than to ever say "I am so sorry I did that to you" or ever ask for *your forgiveness*. It's love & respect for all... but only performatively!

This is why I have been so vocal about manipulation and listening to your own intuition lately. Don't fall for personas. It's not hard to "sound good" on video and in writing in this abundant information & AI age. I've straight up seen other ppl's styles & mannerisms copied

Go off of how they are in their *closest relationships* IRL. Not their "here when it's time for a good time" friends but their family, siblings, past relationships, etc. Most abuse is endured behind closed doors & ppl can be good at hiding it with a shiny front & Twitter persona

Sharing bc I've fallen for manipulation before & I don't want ppl to go through what I've gone through 

Look for actions that match words and principles consistently & respect that is broadly (*not* selectively) applied across ppl they know IRL, accountability, and no hypocrisy

But I remember studying my face and body in the mirror before killing the trip. I would constantly study my face and other features growing up going "maybe I was meant to be a man, I will never be princessy, I will never be allowed to be like other girls" bc I couldn't dress and express how I wanted to and my mom's occasional, derogatory comments about my appearance

Sharing some basic LA fire/outage preparedness things:
1) You can use your car to charge your phone!! freaked out this morning bc phone was dead i was like "how do i know what's going on/if i have to evacuate" and there was still no power/dead phone/no comm with the outside world…

Sharing some basic LA fire/outage preparedness things:
1) You can use your car to charge your phone!! freaked out this morning bc phone was dead i was like "how do i know what's going on/if i have to evacuate" and there was still no power/dead phone/no comm with the outside world
1b) Gather phone, laptop, ID, medications, pets, etc and pack an evacuation bag in case; keep it in your car or on you
1c) get enough charge to make an evacuation plan with friends in the area + safe zones in case you need somewhere to go. (DM if you don't have anyone! this can be scary)
2) This app will tell you real-time evacuation notice and fire status: https://t.co/vh4u5n3wYP
3) Call 211 if you get an evacuation notice and have no where to stay. They've partnered with AirBnb to offer free stays for a week for ppl who have had to evacuate
4) Lots of coffee shops and businesses in the area here and there still have power for food, wifi, charging devices, etc. just call ahead to confirm

Whoaaa okay, I was just cleaning out my voice note app and found a recording of part of an argument from summer 2022. Played it back out of sheer curiosity and even expected to feel validated - only to listen to it, tear up, and see all the ways I was immature af that I couldn't…

Whoaaa okay, I was just cleaning out my voice note app and found a recording of part of an argument from summer 2022. Played it back out of sheer curiosity and even expected to feel validated - only to listen to it, tear up, and see all the ways I was immature af that I couldn't see back then.

I sound so self-righteous in the recording (I mean ofc it's a two-way street & so does the other person to an extent) and I remember when I played it back in 2022 I felt so self-righteous. 

But now, listening to it ~2 years later? I found myself empathizing with the other person. Back then I could only hear the fear in my own voice and I feared the other person's - now I can actually hear the pain in the other person's voice so clearly too. 

In short, we were in a gridlock while asking the other person to understand our triggers and change their behavior. I don't know what it would have taken for one or both of us to just say "I know it matters to you, so for you I can and will try. Can we try for each other?" (back then that would have been near impossible given how we were) but it's how I would have handled any similar scenario now.

Comparative suffering in a relational context simply doesn't make sense to me anymore. You can both be suffering in your own ways and both are valid. Someone can break their arm while another person stubs their toe and they both still hurt. What is important is for both parties to honor each other regardless (not just one; the standard is that the other person is willing to honor you and try too, ofc). I've come to realize this in different contexts & in different kinds of relationships since then.

The takeaway is being able to see myself two years later and go "damn, in this moment I really was just as mistaken as you". It's not something I could see in 2022, or even 2023.

Thank gosh for growing tf up. Bc holy shit do I just not sound like that anymore. While I listened to it, it hurt. It was witnessing the way fractures like this just compound over time while understanding so, so much more now. It's watching yourself make mistakes that you know how to not make now. But it's also a relief to know that I can actually *see* my own mistakes as clear as day now too, and I can empathize with someone I villainized at points. It's a relief to see that I *do* grow up.

I have made a lot of mistakes in my lifetime. Am I the only one in my relationships that has? Helllll no. But I do have my share of mistakes. And I will make more in the future, but hopefully with a willingness to own them and find harmony. We're about to enter the 30s with more wisdom and compassion...lfg :)

i have this particular skill where i can pick out spices and flavor notes used in cooking and baking. thinking of making a visual guide with mnemonics and heuristics for flavor differentiation and intuition when it comes to tasting and cooking. does one exist already for curries?… https://t.co/32grMEYBfp

i have this particular skill where i can pick out spices and flavor notes used in cooking and baking. thinking of making a visual guide with mnemonics and heuristics for flavor differentiation and intuition when it comes to tasting and cooking. does one exist already for curries? what about for baking?

things i can do easily + use cases:
1) being able to identify the combination of spices used in curries or instantly tell if something is missing altogether/too much/too little for dishes that i didn't make or don't know the recipe for
2) taste notes in foods like cheese, coffee, and desserts, taste the difference between notes that many might find similar like lemon and lime, brie and camembert, lavender and rose (also side note: i LOVE florals in desserts. loveee :D)
3) intuitively pair flavors together
4) improvise recipes and make fusion foods by threading common notes and complementary flavor profiles/doing a substitutions that work and make something both familiar and novel

i learned a lot by improvising American-palette Thai fusion peanut noodles when i was a scrappy college student and didn't know how to cook and kept forgetting to do groceries, and had to sub in ingredients often. play around enough with sriracha (spicy)/lime (sour)/soy sauce (salt)/garlic powder (herb and spice)/peanut butter (umami)/honey (sweet)/red chili flakes (more spicy) etc but often having to sub sriracha, brown sugar, ginger, etc. it was some forgiving, fool-proof recipe where you could play around like it was some stat wheel and learn balance first hand. if i'm on a time crunch for a party i make these noodles and people are usually like "these are BOMB" and it's like a diff recipe every time 😆
also i learn a lot by just reading the ingredient labels in niche packaged foods and snacks where my brain starts to remember what goes with what, or remembering what's listed in the ingredients on a menu line item. i think a large chunk of it is verbal retention.

for the longest time i was putting the experience of flavors in foods into words, and then at a random coffee shop months ago i saw this wheel for coffee (below) and i know of Salt, Fat, Acid, and Heat (haven't read it though) so ofc heuristics already exist!

on top of this, my brain absolutely loves to make mnemonic and poetic associations where food looks like how it tastes. eg. cumin is a brown powder so it's earthy, lemon is yellow and bright so it's floral while lime is green and grassy and floral loves flowers while grass loves earth, etc etc. i have categorizations of feminine/masculine, light/dark, soft/strong when it comes to deconstructing and putting together flavors too

feel free to share resources or visuals that are related, or your own experience!

i learned a lot by improvising American-palette Thai fusion peanut noodles when i was a scrappy college student and didn't know how to cook and kept forgetting to do groceries, and had to sub in ingredients often. play around enough with sriracha (spicy)/lime (sour)/soy sauce (salt)/garlic powder (herb and spice)/peanut butter (umami)/honey (sweet)/red chili flakes (more spicy) etc but often having to sub brown sugar, ginger, vinegar, Mae Ploy, etc. it was some forgiving, fool-proof recipe where you could play around like it was some stat wheel and learn balance first hand. if i'm on a time crunch for a party i make these noodles and people are usually like "these are BOMB" and it's like a diff recipe every time 😆
also i learn a lot by just reading the ingredient labels in niche packaged foods and snacks where my brain starts to remember what goes with what, or remembering what's listed in the ingredients on a menu line item. i think a large chunk of it is verbal retention.

for the longest time i was putting the experience of flavors in foods into words, and then at a random coffee shop months ago i saw this wheel for coffee (below) and i know of Salt, Fat, Acid, and Heat (haven't read it though) so ofc heuristics already exist!

on top of this, my brain absolutely loves to make mnemonic and poetic associations where food looks like how it tastes. eg. cumin is a brown powder so it's earthy, lemon is yellow and bright so it's floral while lime is green and grassy and floral loves flowers while grass loves earth, etc etc. i have categorizations of feminine/masculine, light/dark, soft/strong when it comes to deconstructing and putting together flavors too

feel free to share resources or visuals that are related, or your own experience!

i think the "confidence is quiet, arrogance is loud" saying is obtuse and overlooks the loudness of the people bearing their souls trying to express, seeking connection in earnest, wanting so deeply to be seen and combat the loneliness they feel in their soul. it overlooks…

i think the "confidence is quiet, arrogance is loud" saying is obtuse and overlooks the loudness of the people bearing their souls trying to express, seeking connection in earnest, wanting so deeply to be seen and combat the loneliness they feel in their soul. it overlooks exuberance and excitement, and that subtle voice that calls out into the void: "...is there anyone else out there like me? does anyone else get this? does anyone else want to celebrate this with me? what do y'all think of this?" this expression is how we connect.

and the truth is, confidence is quiet. once you feel fulfilled and you find who or what is meant for you, and you feel seen and secure, you might grow into that quieter phase where the desire to broadcast yourself levels out because you know who around you understands your soul and you don't need to send out the beacon to find them. but until then, not all loudness is arrogance. there's absolutely nothing wrong with switching on the lighthouse and seeing who may pull into your harbor

i think the "confidence is quiet, arrogance is loud" saying is obtuse and overlooks the loudness of the people bearing their souls trying to express, seeking connection in earnest, wanting so deeply to be seen and combat the loneliness they feel in their soul. it overlooks exuberance and excitement, and that subtle voice that calls out into the void: "...is there anyone else out there like me? does anyone else get this? does anyone else want to celebrate this with me? what do y'all think of this?" this expression is how we connect. and there's no shame in loneliness; we, as a species, are wired for connection and community.

and the truth is, confidence is quiet. and "confidence" isn't some sweeping label; you can be confident about a lot of personal attributes but you may not have that confidence in your environment or who fills it. once you feel fulfilled and you find who or what is meant for you, and you feel seen and secure, you might grow into that quieter phase where the desire to broadcast yourself levels out because you know who around you understands your soul and you don't need to send out the beacon to find them. but until then, not all loudness is arrogance. there's absolutely nothing wrong with switching on the lighthouse and seeing who may pull into your harbor

i think the "confidence is quiet, arrogance is loud" saying is obtuse and overlooks the loudness of the people bearing their souls trying to express, seeking connection in earnest, wanting so deeply to be seen and combat the loneliness they feel in their soul. it overlooks exuberance and excitement, and that subtle voice that calls out into the void: "...is there anyone else out there like me? does anyone else get this? does anyone else want to celebrate this with me? what do y'all think of this?" this expression is how we connect. and there's no shame in loneliness; we, as a species, are wired for connection and community. it's why we have birthday parties and book clubs, watch movies together instead of by ourselves, fall in love, invite friends over for dinner, remember the little things so we can surprise loved ones.

and the truth is, confidence is quiet. and "confidence" isn't some sweeping label; you can be confident about a lot of personal attributes but you may not have that confidence in your environment or who fills it. once you feel fulfilled and you find who or what is meant for you, and you feel seen and secure, you might grow into that quieter phase where the desire to broadcast yourself levels out because you know who around you understands your soul and you don't need to send out the beacon to find them. but until then, not all loudness is arrogance. there's absolutely nothing wrong with switching on the lighthouse and seeing who may pull into your harbor

i don't destroy cobwebs and i don't play god
neither do you
i can't tell you anymore who is the butterfly and who is the spider
because we are both

i see you resting
your web glistens
with dew drops in the sun

Often peoples' freest tendencies confer the cutting edge of a double-edged sword. Your strengths that stand out in a social group also confer an equal and opposite weakness; your light has a shadow

I got to see one of Picasso's oldest paintings at a museum in Barcelona today, painted when he was just 15 years old. I imagine ppl may see his later work without even realizing how technically talented and versatile he is

I found myself thinking that learning the rules before breaking them is based as hell. I assumed that he gave himself the permission to play in life, freely and by his own rules with a playful "idgaf" when it came to others' dismissal because he's simply not operating on a rubric that they even created or can be the judge of. And that those who don't realize he's based are baseless and that in and of itself must be kind of fun

However, I read up on his personal life and the dark side of this is too much "idgaf"; Picasso was extremely charismatic and invigorating to others, but dismissive and destructive in his personal relationships with people because he so deeply played by his own rules

If you can straight up play in life by your own rules while still inherently honoring and respect other people, you're golden - but I imagine this is easier said than done. I'm not saying it's the price for "greatness" by any means rather than saying it's a consideration for humanization if your play is to be truly playful, harmless, joyful, and free

Do you ever find yourself existing in a little self precipitated out of a higher self state, completely sober, and have entire conversations? It happened this morning. Total nervous system calm. I've otherwise only experienced this on  psychedelics.

I hadn't even meditated for a while but the whole thing started with this deep felt sense of "I love everyone and everyone loves meeee" but then got trippy

 I felt a smaller version of me was asking "if this is love and thriving beyond just survival already, what does it mean to want a relationship or kids anymore? because I still do sometimes, why?" and a larger voice/version was assuredly answering "because you'd make a good lover and a good mom and you want to see it happen. It's living to a potential"
"I'd make a good lover but I'd make a *great* mom. But it's fine if not"
"Yep"
"But I really would"
"Yes, you would"
"But it's okay if not"
"Yes, it is"

And the smaller version of me almost cried because it felt a little jarred about identity thinking "who is this person that was so used to loving someone that doesn't love any *one* now? I don't recognize her. She's always wanted to fall in love soooo badly" before the larger version went "you're not defined by who you love or what you do. You exist in love"

And the whole time all of me felt so calm?? It felt like tripping while just walking around the house on a workday and dissipated later

Again I haven't meditated in a while, but I think I'm getting better sleep and I've healed a lot. And spontaneous contemplation had me separating out into this. It was like this assured conversation between two different knowing versions of myself?? And my body felt NO tension that it otherwise often feels from work stress (or in the past, grief, anger, clinging, anxiety)

...before I got back to work on system design documentation haha

I have what most ppl would consider adhd but I'm focusing on an integrated approach to address it. Sharing a little ritual that i found effective today (10-15 min long) if this helps anyone who struggles w focused completion of work tasks like me and is wanting to try alternative…

I have what most ppl would consider adhd but I'm focusing on an integrated approach to address it. Sharing a little ritual that i found effective today (10-15 min long) if this helps anyone who struggles w focused completion of work tasks like me and is wanting to try alternative methods instead of Adderall or prescription meds and etc.

Context: I sat down today wanting to meditate on a presence i felt yesterday for insights about my future but I still had a lot of work to make up; the tension in my traps told me to address the stress around pending tasks in open awareness. As I do often have my imagination dragging me out of my body to escape thinking about mundane tasks, I knew I had to have a grounding focus in this sit

Ritual:
- light a pretty lotus candle at a mini home altar and set intention (it's always the same, which is "know truth, out of love for myself, for the benefit of all beings" h/t to Hareesh)
- burn root chakra incense (cedar and sandalwood notes)
- because I am hellaaa out of practice I literally put on a 10 min free open awareness meditation from this free app called Medito (which I love btw!!) knowing that I might start to ignore the guide later 
- sat in open awareness and drifted from the guide to ground senses in the incense scent and feel into the base of spine to fully ground
- this is where it goes a little Aletheia, and I'm able to speak to the tension in my traps, recognizing she's a frantic child running around saying "I can't do it" "this [day job] isn't what I'm meant to do, I can't do it" etc etc
- I tell her it's ok that she feels that but I know she can, I'll sit with her, and invite her to come sit with me ("let's help our teammates" tbh I find talking to these parts like they're children and with positive incentive is wayyy more effective than threats or anxious thoughts like "you gotta do this or they'll be mad")
- stayed in this ritual past the medito guided meditation which I wasn't following anyway, just helped as a primer
- extinguish candle but let incense burn bc cedar + sandalwood smell incredible :D and to help me stay grounded 

It was super effective in helping me focus and knock out work after. In that integration I have this visual of repeatedly gently tugging a restless child to sit back down w me and focus.

I've learned that a practice doesn't mean some steady, cosmic progression. In some periods of life you'll be out of practice. Many days the mundane absolutely needs tending to and it might not be what you expect but you'll find it's what you need. Also it's totally ok to be out of practice and use apps or other cues (incense is a sensory cue!!) to reorient and make up your own rituals. Hopefully parts of this are helpful to someone! I'm experimenting with integrative techniques all the time these days

- i'm an engineer. not in any industry that LA is "known for" - not in energy work, healing, yoga, coaching, therapy, etc lol
- i have a STEM and scientific research background and i am the type to read up on papers and corroborate spirituality with science
- i AM spiritual and this word is used a LOT in LA. my version of it: i believe in nonduality - brahman/ultimate reality/unified consciousness. but it's a philosophy + existential framework that's been developed over 13 years and best described as "Advaita Vedanta by way of Tao" (nondual Shaiva Tantra is highly aligned too) and it's derived from personal synthesis and practice that follows: quantum classes in high school > determinism > write poetry to cope with determinism > contemplative ontology > construct panentheism, reject determinism and materialism and opt into idealism > practice meditation > take psychedelics and ask consciousness all these questions to fill in the framework > understand ego dissolution by reading papers on LSD dosage vs. quantitative and qualitative effects as well as other psychedelics and other altered states of consciousness > apply understanding and experience to meditation and practice > all the while cross-checking this with quantum physics > catch up on physics now to be able to talk to skeptics better and relate it to science (even tho i personally don't believe materialism is all there is it helps to explain the science of it)...etc etc

You can say you're practicing for the right intentions and so purely and wholly even believe in it, but the intention of practicing for power is so, so sneaky. There's a subtle "I can do things that people don't even know I can do" or "I can see and know things" is powerful and…

You can say you're practicing for the right intentions and so purely and wholly even believe in it, but the intention of practicing for power is so, so sneaky. There's a subtle "I can do things that people don't even know I can do" or "I can see and know things" is powerful and even addictive, but in there is an attachment to having an advantage and even comparison or competition. It's subtle but it's there. 

Sometimes it's better to sit down and go back to not even trying to know. To not focus on it. Don't try to "see", just "be". You don't need to know, just exist. It existing with Tao vs. conviction beyond the present. The sneaky, subtle ego trying to play the role of a goddess until you actually call upon a goddess for protection because you've invited demons (and thankfully, she does come) because you decided to enter the arena and play these games out of pure curiosity and intrigue, but then a high.

If you choose to play a game, make sure you believe in something higher than yourself like a Divine Mother because thinking that you have become her will be your downfall. Better yet is to be at peace knowing a game exists, but you don't have to play it to exist with peace. You have everything you need

if you attend purpose-driven community events and you're looking to get into community hosting, i recently discovered that it's so worth it to connect with the venue owners at the event itself. i used to be so shy to talk to people i'd never met about ideas i had! but then i…

if you attend purpose-driven community events and you're looking to get into community hosting, i recently discovered that it's so worth it to connect with the venue owners at the event itself. i used to be so shy to talk to people i'd never met about ideas i had! but then i realized these are self-selected pools of people where those showing up care about community and connection, and so do those who lend their space, and they'll often get excited with you about said ideas/support your events.

eg. yesterday i discovered a cozy coffee cart-meets-purpose-driven third space in LA with great community vibes at a candlelit meditation + talk event lead by monks affiliated with my friend's temple and got to talk to the owner of the space. thrilled to help run a community art therapy event there :D

examples of events that lend themselves well to this environment: free/donation-based yoga/meditation/mindfulness events, events that are run in partnership with local buddhist or hindu temples that are open to the public, smaller local charity events, open mic events, local artist showcase events, etc. i'm sure there are more

if you have the means, ofc, do donate or support the business hosting these types of events! my favorite thing about being in a huge city like LA is all these local third space bubbles - i love this trend of small businesses supporting community hosting. it's a win-win when ppl naturally buy the coffee bc it tastes great, the owner is awesome, and he does so much meaningful work an is there at these community events as well

There's such a duality to the heavy rains in LA. It's healing for the earth after a fire season and it's a gorgeous time to live in the valley because the mountains become a lush green with life, but it also floods often here. And then I think about the homeless population that's out in this weather while I romanticize falling asleep to the sound of it in my room; I imagine torrential downpour sounds different while in a tent. I used to feel guilty but now it's like I hold and observe a lot of perspectives at once

prefacing this with how it's just my take first of all!

but think about how when you were a kid, early on, you do something you feel is cool and you'd probably want your caregivers to notice or your parents to be proud of you. kids want to feel seen, like they're cared about, and they can do things successfully. they're inherently not as self-validating as adults. adults are more self-validating, but some social validation is still healthy, normal, and necessary to an extent. if someone is seeking a *ton* of social validation or praise they're making their self-esteem heavily a function of other ppls' opinions and reactions; therefore it comes across as less emotionally mature. if you're responsible for your own self-esteem you have a healthy self-esteem first and you cling less to ppls' reactions than someone with lower self-esteem; it's less "neediness" that can come across as more childish when you're interacting with other adults who may not seek as much validation back. many adults are happy to be validating to other adults but to the degree that's appropriate for friendships/partnerships rather than parent-child and others don't like being this way at all (but that's a whole other thing haha)

trigger warning: child abuse, domestic violence, abuse

i have the backstory of someone that totally could have had a jaded "i'm done with men" mentality except i adore the men that i'm around and this is a post of appreciation for the genuinely kind men out here. like legitimately surrounded by kings only. ages 19-29 were a string of long, abusive relationships (physical and verbal aggression, violence, intimidation, control, all one-sided etc...and a whole lot of other things i don't want to get into).

my dad is actually a caring dad and my younger brother is my bestie for life. ironically, my mom is the one that was abusive as hell (physically, verbally, psychologically) and that's where i learned all the "chase after an abuser's love" patterns - dw, unlearned it all now. and i set my boundaries with her. all to say, my family relationships are chill now. but growing up, i didn't have a reference point for standards for men and they'd treat me like absolute shit. i was in STEM environments where subtly misogyny was rampant. i'd be like "well, idk if there's nice men that don't yell or throw their arms around or swear or use slurs when they're angry. maybe just my brother, but i practically raised that kid and he loves me a ton. so he doesn't count." (my dad actually cares about the person i am and is mostly sweet and goofy and a hippie, but he would still yell sometimes for lil things and i've grown up watching my parent's fights...holy sh*t lol)

BUT THEN i made so many genuinely kind male friends through the years and they're gems, and they've showed me what's possible in male personalities. sometimes i tear up repeatedly affirming this belief in goodness bc i see it in y'all ALL THE TIME. as much as i want marriage and kids, i'd rather die surrounded by friends and unmarried with no kids (i don't think i'll die alone per se lol) than end up with a man that experiences rage and takes it out on his girlfriend or wife. i hate to speak in extremes, but i feel so strongly now that i'd rather LEAVE (first choice, and now i know how) or die (but leave first, ofc) than tolerate a man yelling at me or throwing his arms around or punching objects in front of me or shaking me by the shoulders or slamming me down on a surface or punching down next to my head or shoving me EVER again. the kings around me would NEVER.

and i'm not scared to date again at all. i feel ready for it after taking almost a year being single to genuinely heal from all of this. i know my social safety nets are strong and friends WILL check me on this shit but even deeper than that is standards that i refuse to waver on and discernment that won't tolerate BS ever again.

from the bottom of my heart, thank you to the men that put the gentle in gentlemen. and forever and always, thank you to the kindness of women. it's all been insanely healing. i have a lump in my throat and i'm almost tearing up typing this. you have no idea.

once upon a time (6+ years ago) i didn't rly have any friends after moving coasts, and i had a lot of social anxiety. when friends who i admired took a long time to get back to me, i'd worry they didn't like me and i'd take it deeply personally. and even then, i also struggled to…

once upon a time (6+ years ago) i didn't rly have any friends after moving coasts, and i had a lot of social anxiety. when friends who i admired took a long time to get back to me, i'd worry they didn't like me and i'd take it deeply personally. and even then, i also struggled to find interesting conversational partners. my friend circles gradually changed and so did i; i learned myself better, and i said goodbye to social anxiety entirely ~2-3 years ago. i still empathize with what it's like to have social anxiety, though. or anxiety in general. when i had it, i had it bad.

i feel like i have a lot of friends and interesting conversations and it's more than i can humanly keep up with digitally. lots of DMs, lots of texts, etc. and i realize how HARD it is to stay consistently digitally connected when you are extremely social and there's a constant influx of messages. other ppl realize this too. i find myself replying to things on a priority order: emergencies with friends and family, time-sensitive scheduling/in-person planning, IRL friends i see more often, and then everything else. i sometimes feel conscious about how i have messages and collabs that i have to take time to get back to but realistically there's also a 9-5 job to do, an offline life to live, etc. it's also like "when do i take a breather and read a book? or cook something nicer and more elaborate than my standard meal prep?"

i've seen people tweak out a little when i get back to them later with like "btw i'm sorry! i wasn't trying to be weird" like there was something wrong with their last message because they read into how long it took to respond. and it's endearing, and a past version of me relates! and also, you're perfectly okay. if you struggle with (social) anxiety, please don't take how long ppl take to respond to casual convo as a statement of you. sometimes it just has to do with bandwidth and juggling a very full offline life. maybe this helps someone that can relate to past me :) i don't think i understood this well until i became *that person who takes a while to reply to casual convo* too
(also i totally needed to write this out for myself too because i want ppl to know that i still get it and i still care)

Hi! I'm Nadia and I'm searching for a life partner to start a loving family with 💕 I've made so many incredible friends through twitter connections over my ~2 active years here, so I recently thought, "why not life partner too?"

A bit about me:
- I just turned 30
- I'm 5' 5.5" tall
- I work full-time as an engineer in the medical device industry. Will I do this forever? Not sure! I have my own ambitions but for now I love the stability and work-life balance in life
- I'm creatively inclined. Drawing, painting, and writing are some of my passions! 
- On twitter I come off as sensitive and introverted so one thing that surprises ppl IRL is how social and outgoing I am in person. I balance work, hobby, play, and socializing
- I introspect, reflect, and iterate a lot on myself but not at the expense of liking myself and where I'm at too
- tbh I'm some odd blend of social, bubbly, friendly, normie to the twitterverse but nerdy to the normieverse - but it works for me and I love allll kinds of ppl!!
- ..but I'm introverted (no one believes me) bc I can have a wonderful party in my head while solo-strolling around a city for hours exploring
- I don't fit neatly into boxes. (Honestly most ppl don't?) Like you can catch me being chill with friends doing wholesome things looking girly and demure or going to the occasional high-energy music festival dressed like a gothic fairy degenerate (see below)
- I value taking care of mental and physical health and living a balanced life
- I consider myself spiritual. Advaita Vedanta, non-dual Shaiva/Shakti tantra, and Daoism all resonate with me
- I LOL at the meme that says "intelligent, good looking, and emotionally stable...pick two" because then what's the point of working on yourself? I don't think in life that one has to pick only two 🤪 do you?
- ppl keep nicely telling me I am more attractive in person than in photos so for this post I said "ok lemme make half of these videos??" IDK how to pose and I get bashful about posing (as you can see below) so just take the damn raw videos y'all LOL

Interested, or have a friend who would be? Consider taking or sharing my dating compatibility survey!

To save you time, *don't* take the survey in the next post if you:
- don't want kids in the next 5 years (I want kids and in 5 years I'd be 35)
- are looking for casual fun (I'm a lover girl at heart, I like dating with intention, and I'm looking to find the one)
- aren't over your ex or something/aren't actually ready to date (go heal homie! I believe in you)

Some other things to be mindful about (strong preferences):
- I tend to be attracted to taller men (I'm nearly 5'6" and tend to strongly, strongly prefer 5'10" or taller) BUT I could be open-minded if a lot of other things about you check out 
- I'm physically fit/lift, take care of my brain and body, and I prefer the same in a partner 
- I prefer my male partner to be older than me, so strong preference for those born 1994 or earlier (I'm 1995) BUT I could be open-minded to a couple of years younger
- if I cloned my financial situation into another person, we could comfortably afford a house and kids tomorrow (not that kids are coming tomorrow..hell no LOL) but ask yourself if you can meet or exceed that financial standard :)
- I get along with both spiritual and non-spiritual ppl but for life partners I might go best w someone who believes that *something* is there! Open-minded about this for now though

(Also thank you to my friends Kat and Christine for being my hypewomen in one of these videos 😂 I was getting self-conscious about posing in 40°F without layers)

i'm getting asked a lot in both IRL and on here "why are going with this unconventional dating process? why don't you just meet people IRL?" and here's what i have to say about it:

1) i do meet ppl IRL, but in the dating sense, they often haven't been my type so far. i live in east LA. maybe if i was in SF or a techie/nerdy interests-concentrated city it would be a little different. i love LA, but many guys i meet in the party scene (usually there to hang with my friends) don't give me husband vibes haha. i appreciate the qualities of being social and lively, but also genuinely kind, intentional, and curious
2) anon pfp doesn't mean ppl are unattractive. and being chronically online doesn't mean ppl lack social skills IRL. my non-twitter IRL friends and ppl who haven't ever met mutuals often assume these things, but i've met twitter ppl offline and many are attractive and socially adept! by and large, most ppl from here are extremely social and *great* conversationalists IRL
3) i love convos with ppl on twitter but idk anything about y'all when your pfp is an AI-generated piece of art. and i have no inkling of whether or not we're long-term compatible, hence survey questions. "why don't you just DM ppl like a normal person?" because it takes SO long, i'm not trying to interview ppl, and i can barely keep up with my *friends* over text and i got so much to do offline lol

i'm pretty unapologetic about stating my preferences for dating, y'all. but sometimes, IRL and online, i see/intuit how having a height preference discourages some men and i never want this to be a statement of a man's worth, attractiveness, or masculinity because it's NONE of this. i've had situations where ppl have shot their shot and with great confidence but they're just not fully flipping the visceral switches in my brain, and i can sense the confusion and deflation in them. i'm usually uplifting online and to my friends IRL, so i feel sympathetic when i see peoples' energies sink and i want to say something. i have friends that are shorter than me but attractive and i *know* would make incredible partners when they find the woman for them.

my 5'0" and 5'2" friends can say "i prefer at least 5'8" to date" and ppl don't flinch. that's 6+ inches to their height. know what happens when i add 6 inches to *my* height? why do they get to do that?

i'm 5'5.5" so it's statistically plausible that i'd prefer around 6'0" (see the distributions below) but i KNOW that if i said THAT number in my post i'd get a lot more destructive comments and that's not even the experiential threshold i personally need to feel dainty, cute, and tiny compared to my male partner (which is what i want, leave me alone, i like this feeling and it's what *i want* and when it's a big, open world and i'm single... why preemptively compromise?)... based on my personal experiences, 5'10" (with a little bit of wiggle room) works for me here; any shorter and i will very likely get wishy washy, where i recognize someone's rizz and attractiveness, but i don't feel internally in my full feminine because of the smaller size differential between us, and this confusion is not fun for you or me. there are some objectively very attractive men under this height but the switch in my brain doesn't fully flip to "hell yeah!!!". this is based off of IRL experiences and how attraction works in my brain. i don't have complete control over attraction; it's either fully there, partially there, or not there at all. there's thresholds for it and i'm the pattern-recognition type that can quantify it and i'm not rly afraid to vocalize it for honesty and efficiency's sake.

low-key, men have physical preferences too. when i talk to some guys, they confirm this. a lot of taller guys like taller women, and some tall guys like really short women and i'm borderline *too tall for them* even though they're technically tall enough for my height preferences...and i don't take that personally LOL. everyone has preferences. everyone.

i'm ALWAYS towering over my female friends (5'0"-5'3" women, usually). there are so many beautiful, awesome women who are shorter than me. maybe they're perfect for the beautiful, awesome men that are shorter than my *personal preferences* as a woman on the taller side. i am not a teeny tiny woman, okay? i am not.

my shortest female friend and tallest male friend are getting married soon and they're cute af; i LOVE them together and they are perfect for each other. zendaya is taller than tom holland, benny blanco is shorter than selena gomez. there are always exceptions to the rule, but people also have preferences. it's not that complicated, damn. just go find the one for you, this is a world of billions.

i'm just ONE random person in this world with my own backstory, and what i like, and my preferences, but it says absolutely *nothing* about your worth and capability to attract women, king. nothing. if i see you in passing i might still be like "dang, what a beautiful person". i think this quite often, actually, and it's not even appearance-based for most people!! but you don't need to be everyone in the world's type (physically, mentally, emotionally); you'll be the type for the one that you're going to end up with and perfect in every way for *her* :)
