i feel like a lot of ppl here think i have autistic-coded tweets or think i'm autistic; IRL *now* not many non-twitter ppl think this. i'm not autistic, but i vibe w autistic ppl (and non-autistic ppl!). i probably developed differently because of severe, control-induced gender…

i feel like a lot of ppl here think i have autistic-coded tweets or think i'm autistic; IRL *now* not many non-twitter ppl think this. i'm not autistic, but i vibe w autistic ppl (and non-autistic ppl!). i probably developed differently because of severe, control-induced gender dysmorphia. life shifted when i could actually make and retain female friends. see ~the lore below~. i get along wonderfully with neurodivergent ppl. i love black sheep, i love awkward, i love shy, i love weird - i identify with a lot of it and empathize with ALL of it. i also get along great with neurotypical ppl, and i do have a ton of social fluidity. i'm pretty strong about inclusivity and nonjudgement as values.

here's the relevant lore:
1) one of my closest, consistent caretakers growing up was my neurodivergent grandmother. she has schizoaffective bipolar but it was undiagnosed until i was 10 years old. between ages 1-3 i'd spend all hours of the day with her while my parents were at work. off meds she was incredibly artistic and poetic, and a phenomenal storyteller. childhood was FILLED with art and writing. i'd just sit on the floor of her room and draw all day while she'd tell me folktales and draw with me. but psychotic breaks are confusing to witness alone as a toddler (won't get into it), where you're like "*shrug* this is happening, idk what it is, but i love you". lots of family unrest in the house. (i'm glazing over a lottt with other family members here.) when she had to be medicated my family said it was better for everyone but it kinda broke my heart in ways and kid me disagreed. a lot of the creative and active sides of her brain subsided. i think this is why i hold creativity and play so dearly as values. i was pretty animated and creative as a kid and loved to make up games for friends. i'd "lead" a lot of games. (i think all of this has come back as an adult, btw :D)
2) i wanted to be girly early on but my mom was severely controlling and shamed a lot of feminine things because of her religious beliefs that she applied in a rly *authoritarian* fashion. she also totally has NPD, lots of physical and emotional abuse in childhood for even attempting to be myself or stand up for myself. i'm at peace with it and i accept her as she is, long story etc etc but she's not changing and i'm grown, and it's all chill. i wanted to dress like girls my age at every stage of life but i was forced to dress masculine and conservatively. i don't want this to be a whole mommy issues post but the bullying of many physical aspects by my own mom (voice, hair, walk, body, other features) and abuse and weird lil things like cutting off all my hair without permission was so severe that i just felt like garbage and i was like "maybe i should be a boy but i really wish i was a girl" and it hit hard bc when i was young i thought my mom was sooo beautiful and i adored her. i wanted to fit in so badly but my thoughts were like "maybe i should give up and be a transgender man, even though i don't really want to be - i'll never make it as a girl, i'm not allowed to be like that" (i *am* a ciswoman btw). i didn't fit in at school and i hid that the reason was "bc my mom is really muslim" bc at the time there was so much severe islamophobia in the news and i felt severe shame about that too. (btw - this is not a general statement about muslim parents. many sweet muslim parents exist, it's not the religion's fault per se.) just...grew up shrunken, sad, controlled. didn't fit in, felt weird, played neopets all day (lots of original art and writing) bc i could never go to parties or friends house or leave the house bc of severe control (or i could but it was like...2-4 times a year?) and became veryyyy internet native. getting to go to a friend's house felt like i won the lottery that day. it was like...going to disneyland. 
3) 2013: escaped all of that in college where moved out and lived on campus. i finally got to start wearing skirts and a crop top.. i had like ONE crop top for those 4 years LOL. but i struggled to understand how to put on my makeup for half of college; couldn't fit in with other girls, couldn't "get in" to girl groups, didn't fit into drinking and partying culture early when i wanted to fit in. had a lot of male friends, though. [redacted bad stuff blah blah there's a lot here and it is pretty bad, but healed from it :) just don't wanna share]. i got into a rly bad, controlling 6-year relationship with a man that shared control mechanisms with my mom (repetitive compulsion, yay) and it resulted in continued low self-esteem and no real friends. (btw that relationship was shit lol)
4) 2017: grad school it was just me; LDR with the person above. every friday night i'd practice learning how to do my makeup better, eventually wanting to fit in with "the cool girls with perfect makeup". zero female role models, but wanted so badly to be feminine, and just watched a lot of IG videos and youtube. i finally started making female friends.
5) 2018: moved out west to rejoin that bf on the west cost, and it was a crappy, misogynistic engineering bro friend group. became tiny and shruken again - him yelling and being physically aggressive scared me into submission QUICK. i was one of two girls (the gfs) in that group and we neverrr got speaking time socially. that bf could just raise his voice and i'd go into quiet tears and stop making my point and obey (LOL INSANE I KNOW)
6) COVID happens; at the same time i make one more female friend and start opening up to the one other girl in that controlled friend group, and they point-blank just tell me to leave the relationship.
7) i leave, year's 2020. move out to a place where there's only one female roommate, start making female friends during the pandemic using bumble bff, low-key "learning how to girl" on fast learning mode. successfully do this. 
8) spent 2020-2022 in and out of diff friend groups, but finally solidify my core female friend group. in 2021 on my first psychedelic experience i remember looking in the mirror, coming out, and having an anxious moment repeatedly saying to my friend "i just wanna be a girl" and she said "you ARE a girl" and something after that trip clicked. i was like "wait, i AM a woman". 
9) soooo everything from becoming fully feminine like i always wanted to be and socializing with ppl and all that is only 5 years old, yes. but i do it well. and seamlessly. i was awkward for a long time but the turning point was 2020-2021, and i do NOT take a long time to learn things :)
10) 2022-present is probably where i just attained full social adeptness

sooo am i autistic? probably not? did i develop differently bc of diff (tbh, abusive and controlling) conditions in a way where i couldn't fit in with others, still? yep. do i have empathy for ppl who feel different or outcasted? 100%, bc i was there. and i have a deep, heartfelt reason for wanting to retain creativity and play in life bc i saw the suppression of it in someone i care a lot about. so i stay silly, poetic, empathetic, nonjudgmental, curious, uplifting, and inclusive in case anyone else needs to know they can and will find acceptance and warmth too. whatever you are or however you grew up, feeling like you belong or deserve to exist as you are shouldn't have to feel hard <3

2) i wanted to be girly early on but my mom was severely controlling and shamed a lot of feminine things because of her religious beliefs that she applied in a rly *authoritarian* fashion. she also totally has NPD, lots of physical and emotional abuse in childhood for even attempting to be myself or stand up for myself. i'm at peace with it and i accept her as she is, long story etc etc but she's not changing and i'm grown, and it's all chill. i wanted to dress like girls my age at every stage of life but i was forced to dress masculine and conservatively. i don't want this to be a whole mommy issues post but the bullying of many physical aspects by my own mom (voice, hair, walk, body, other features) and abuse and weird lil things like cutting off all my hair without permission was so severe that i just felt like garbage and i was like "maybe i should be a boy but i really wish i was a girl" and it hit hard bc when i was young i thought my mom was sooo beautiful and i adored her. i wanted to fit in so badly but my thoughts were like "maybe i should give up and be a transgender man, even though i don't really want to be - i'll never make it as a girl, i'm not allowed to be like that" (i *am* a ciswoman btw). i didn't fit in at school and i hid that the reason was "bc my mom is really muslim" bc at the time there was so much severe islamophobia in the news and i felt severe shame about that too. (btw - this is not a general statement about muslim parents. many sweet muslim parents exist, it's not the religion's fault per se.) just...grew up shrunken, sad, controlled. didn't fit in, felt weird, played neopets all day (lots of original art and writing) bc i could never go to parties or friends house or leave the house bc of severe control (or i could but it was like...2-4 times a year?) and became veryyyy internet native. getting to go to a friend's house felt like i won the lottery that day. it was like...going to disneyland.
 
3) 2013: escaped all of that in college where moved out and lived on campus. i finally got to start wearing skirts and a crop top.. i had like ONE crop top for those 4 years LOL. but i struggled to understand how to put on my makeup for half of college; couldn't fit in with other girls, couldn't "get in" to girl groups, didn't fit into drinking and partying culture early when i wanted to fit in. had a lot of male friends, though. [redacted bad stuff blah blah there's a lot here and it is pretty bad, but healed from it :) just don't wanna share]. i got into a rly bad, controlling 6-year relationship with a man that shared control mechanisms with my mom (repetitive compulsion, yay) and it resulted in continued low self-esteem and no real friends. (btw that relationship was shit lol)

4) 2017: grad school it was just me; LDR with the person above. every friday night i'd practice learning how to do my makeup better, eventually wanting to fit in with "the cool girls with perfect makeup". zero female role models, but wanted so badly to be feminine, and just watched a lot of IG videos and youtube. i finally started making female friends.

5) 2018: moved out west to rejoin that bf on the west cost, and it was a crappy, misogynistic engineering bro friend group. became tiny and shruken again - him yelling and being physically aggressive scared me into submission QUICK. i was one of two girls (the gfs) in that group and we neverrr got speaking time socially. that bf could just raise his voice and i'd go into quiet tears and stop making my point and obey (LOL INSANE I KNOW)

6) COVID happens; at the same time i make one more female friend and start opening up to the one other girl in that controlled friend group, and they point-blank just tell me to leave the relationship.

7) i leave, year's 2020. move out to a place where there's only one female roommate, start making female friends during the pandemic using bumble bff, low-key "learning how to girl" on fast learning mode. successfully do this. 

8) spent 2020-2022 in and out of diff friend groups, but finally solidify my core female friend group. in 2021 on my first psychedelic experience i remember looking in the mirror, coming out, and having an anxious moment repeatedly saying to my friend "i just wanna be a girl" and she said "you ARE a girl" and something after that trip clicked. i was like "wait, i AM a woman". 

9) soooo everything from becoming fully feminine like i always wanted to be and socializing with ppl and all that is only 5 years old, yes. but i do it well. and seamlessly. i was awkward for a long time but the turning point was 2020-2021, and i do NOT take a long time to learn things :)

10) 2022-present is probably where i just attained full social adeptness

sooo am i autistic? probably not? did i develop differently bc of diff (tbh, abusive and controlling) conditions in a way where i couldn't fit in with others, still? yep. do i have empathy for ppl who feel different or outcasted? 100%, bc i was there. and i have a deep, heartfelt reason for wanting to retain creativity and play in life bc i saw the suppression of it in someone i care a lot about. so i stay silly, poetic, empathetic, nonjudgmental, curious, uplifting, and inclusive in case anyone else needs to know they can and will find acceptance and warmth too. whatever you are or however you grew up, feeling like you belong or deserve to exist as you are shouldn't have to feel hard <3

I've done cessation exercises to let go of the grip that my 20s had on me where I felt like I needed to be partnered to feel whole. This helped me feel whole on my own, such that I actually feel ready for partnership, should it happen, while also being at peace if it doesn't.…

I've done cessation exercises to let go of the grip that my 20s had on me where I felt like I needed to be partnered to feel whole. This helped me feel whole on my own, such that I actually feel ready for partnership, should it happen, while also being at peace if it doesn't. Then this question arose: "If you're at peace, why any longing? If you feel complete, why any desire?"

I had to reflect a lot on what I would even experience partnered that I can't already get on my own and the blunt answer is romantic love and spiritually gratifying physical intimacy, and while that sounds like a reductive take, it isn't. A lot of love and sex entails the meeting of polarities. I might feel like we're all fractals of one existence but in any given moment I will be what I am and not be what I am not; I'm complete on my own and so is he but together there's harmony that moves a part of the universe.

Ultimate romance, conceptually, is resonance. I have this visceral thought that goes "I wanna be destroyed". I want to destroy and be destroyed; in mind-blanking moments I want to experience destructive interference like two waveforms meeting and merging into the void when your minds go blank. I want to create and be created; the constructive interference of new life, new consciousness, love to new strengths that I wouldn't otherwise know (true, romantic love and parent/child sound like the kinds of love I have yet to experience).

For now I'm grateful to be able to pour love into everything else around me and myself. Longing is the deep desire to discover what it's like to resonate rather than singularly reverberate. It's the desire to amplify cycles of creation and destruction when you're met, matched, and moved.

at this trance rave two years ago i was dancing up a storm and i was on 🌈 where it just felt like pure joy, equanimity, and bliss. ppl were creeping my space but not actually violating ANY boundary the whole night - literally felt this proprioception and perfect boundaries that were implicit in the way i carried myself. the lesson in a trip right before this one was "ppl will know EXACTLY how much to engage w you vs when to not fk with you - and it's all in how you carry yourself" and it was in effect in this one too.

my (now ex) was being passive aggressive nearly the whole rave and told me "you have demonic energy, it's kind of cool" at one point - he seemed awed and terrified at the same time, a common theme in our relationship (while friends told me i looked like an angel/fairy/goddess hahah). bc on that stuff i often feel hijacked into full integration. at one point in the night, i remember telling him off for being hypocritical and overly critical of me, when at the time i otherwise would have been too scared to say anything if i was sober in fear of being raged at into silence or submission. i remember fully feeling a flash of anger without any self-gaslighting that dissipated immediately after saying what i needed to, and then going *right back* to dancing and vibing with everyone else.

i don't think this moment ever left my head. the lesson of that trip was "okay GOOD i'll be a demon. i'll be self-assured, i'll go IDGAF. channel that. stand up for yourself without doubt."

GO BE AN ANGEL

Out-of-context spiritual platitudes and forgiveness culture can be so toxic and detrimental when it comes to processing abuse and reclaiming your agency.

"I deserved better than my ex. I can do better-"
"Comparison is the thief of joy. You're not better. No one is. Just send…

Out-of-context spiritual platitudes and forgiveness culture can be so toxic and detrimental when it comes to processing abuse and reclaiming your agency.

"I deserved better than my ex. I can do better-"
"Comparison is the thief of joy. You're not better. No one is. Just send love." 

"Fk that person for-"
"You must forgive! You must send love! Don't say that!"

NO. Stop. I healed fully when I leaned into the anger and stopped letting platitudes shut down processing I had to do.

It's natural, normal, healing, and necessary to compare yourself to an ex and say "I can do better. I deserved better."

If you feel pressured by the spiritual community to skip to the "you should forgive and send love" part, you never, ever get to name and process all the abuse. You never get to understand what happened. You never get to reclaim your own worth and dissolve the damage that was done to you by their abuse, and instead cling to narratives spun by all their gaslighting. You end up thinking a relationship failing is all your fault when all you did was show up in goodness. You forget the moments you were in complete tears, tiptoeing on eggshells, while they continued to behave with hypocritical aggression and continue to blame it on you bc "we're all equal and we're all good! Stop! Just send love!" That stuff? That needs rework and processing such that you never, ever settle for something like that agjan. And no, jumping straight to "forgiveness and sending love to them" doesn't cut it for processing that.

So no, comparison isn't the thief of joy here. You were always kinder, more gracious, more patient, more loyal, more devoted. You can do better. You're out of their league when it comes to goodness.

And no, you don't have to forgive unless you feel ready to. You don't have to send love if you don't feel ready to. Not if it makes it seem like you should push away anything that feels like "the opposite" of love and hinder your own healing.

Once you process it and understand you deserve better, that you ARE out of someone's league, and you are entirely worthy of love and all your emotions are valid - then you can reevaluate. And guess what, you still are on the spiritual path the whole time. Until then, don't let platitudes invalidate your necessary processing.

You know, gratitude isn't that hard for me. Every day I feel like I've "already made it" tbh. I grew up soooo lonely and constantly controlled, abused, and bullied at home and then at school, ostracized in college over rumors about things I never did, and controlled and abused by…

You know, gratitude isn't that hard for me. Every day I feel like I've "already made it" tbh. I grew up soooo lonely and constantly controlled, abused, and bullied at home and then at school, ostracized in college over rumors about things I never did, and controlled and abused by (now ex) bfs into severe imposter syndrome and internalized blame.

I don't know what safe, romantic love feels like - at all. I just know the ultimate threat of yelling, aggression, manipulation, and being compared to other women. And I'll never endure that again; I clock these types before knowing them for longer than a few hours. And I would like to be in love, and I'd like to be a parent, but honestly, finally having friends that genuinely and deeply care about me and vice versa such that I don't feel like I'll die alone in my apartment if I get super sick - that feels like enough. I feel like I've already struck gold.

I know it sounds like the bare minimum to some people, but to me it means the world because it's like...only 13% of my 30-year old life that I can say this? I feel a general wealth of love in community that I just didn't have 5 years ago. It's already so much better than any relationship where I was treated like garbage. I think all animals are highly adaptable and the human psyche is no exception. I'm like "it's chill if this life time isn't about romantic love or having kids, for me. Ppl are like "cope" and...I don't even care what it is or what it looks like. "Oh noooo, she found a way to adapt to life and find happiness?" Call it cope, call it constant gratitude. I watch ppl around me fall in love and I'm like "aww that's sweet". I'm curious about if/when/ever it will ever happen for me but also life looks a million times better than it ever did, including when I was in a relationship (but I pick wisely now). So Idc what you want to call it.

I've lived by "No one's coming to save you. You have to make life happen yourself" for so long (and still probably do, to some extent) that now the part of my soul that reminds and whispers "but there are ppl who care enough to help, and that's sweet too" makes my heart smile a little.

There's no losing. Only gratitude.

You know, gratitude isn't that hard for me. Every day I feel like I've "already made it" tbh. I grew up soooo lonely and constantly controlled, abused, and bullied at home and then at school, ostracized in college over rumors about things I never did, developed severe social anxiety, and was then controlled and abused by (now ex) bfs into severe imposter syndrome, nervous system shutdown, and internalized blame.

I don't know what safe, romantic love feels like - at all. I just know the ultimate threat of yelling, aggression, manipulation, and being compared to other women. And I'll never endure that again; I clock these types before knowing them for longer than a few hours. And I would like to be in love, and I'd like to be a parent, but honestly, finally having friends that genuinely and deeply care about me and vice versa such that I don't feel like I'll die alone in my apartment if I get super sick - that feels like enough. I feel like I've already struck gold.

I know it sounds like the bare minimum to some people, but to me it means the world because it's like...only 13% of my 30-year old life that I can say this? I feel a general wealth of love in community that I just didn't have 5 years ago. It's already so much better than any relationship where I was treated like garbage. I think all animals are highly adaptable and the human psyche is no exception. I'm like "maybe this lifetime isn't about romantic love or having kids, for me." Ppl are like "cope" and...I don't even care what it is or what it looks like. "Oh noooo, she found a way to adapt to life and find happiness?" Call it cope, call it constant gratitude. I watch ppl around me fall in love and I'm like "aww that's sweet". I'm curious about if/when/ever it will happen for me but also life looks a million times better than it ever did, including when I was in a relationship (but I pick wisely now). So Idc what you want to call it.

I've lived by "No one's coming to save you. You have to make life happen yourself" for so long (and still probably do, to some extent) that now the part of my soul that reminds and whispers "but there are ppl who care enough to help, and that's sweet too" makes my heart smile a little.

There's no losing. Only gratitude.

Aesthetic conformity is actually somewhat necessary for competence and confidence sometimes before you achieve mastery and break the mold; if you ever gave up on activities bc you weren't allowed to conform as a result of external control, external narratives, or bullying, I…

Aesthetic conformity is actually somewhat necessary for competence and confidence sometimes before you achieve mastery and break the mold; if you ever gave up on activities bc you weren't allowed to conform as a result of external control, external narratives, or bullying, I challenge you to retry it all as an adult with agency! Discover everything you "didn't get to do" as a kid and do it now, but on your terms. I'll explain with an example. 

I played pickleball yesterday for the first time and it was the first competitive sport I played in 15 years (the last time was high school gym class in freshman year !!), and I was actually good at it, and it filled me with joy but made me confront some kind of deep sadness that I avoided and missed out on sports all these years. This avoidance was a result of a controlling, oppressive environment. As a child I was good at sports but gave up quickly on sports as a teenager. Once I looked more "physically mature", my dogmatic, religious, and controlling mother shamed how "skin tight" and "revealing" athletic clothing was; I had to wear baggy clothes, shorts were banned, and I'd be a sweaty mess in 80 degree weather. I'd overheat and I get in my head, and my high school bully would constantly point out how sweaty I was all the time. I started being terrible at sports because I'd just stay stuck in my head about how I just looked like a "sweaty loser" while all the other women my age looked free flowy in their athletic clothes and played freely. My posture was hunched, I had no confidence, I didn't look like anyone else, and I gave up on it all. "I'm not athletic, I'm just gross and sweaty and sluggy. I'll never be free like the other ppl". Why didn't I just secretly change to different clothing that was functional and confirming? Because it was deeply ingrained that there would be severe, aggressive, and abusive consequences at home if she found out.

Fast-forward years later, when I'm scared to try snowboarding at 26 but I do it anyway and I realize "oh, I can play sports." And now at 30, I clocked my fear and reluctance to participate in competitive sports bc I worried about being perceived as a "sluggy person that sucks at everything" - but then adult me traced it back all the way to childhood control and realized none of it exists now. And I had a freakin blast 😃 I can't believe I avoided competitive sports for 15 years but here we are now

Aesthetic conformity is actually somewhat necessary for competence and confidence sometimes before you achieve mastery and break the mold; if you ever gave up on activities bc you weren't allowed to conform as a result of external control, external narratives, or bullying, I…

Aesthetic conformity is actually somewhat necessary for competence and confidence sometimes before you achieve mastery and break the mold; if you ever gave up on activities bc you weren't allowed to conform as a result of external control, external narratives, or bullying, I challenge you to retry it all as an adult with agency! Discover everything you "didn't get to do" as a kid and do it now, but on your terms. I'll explain with an example. 

I played pickleball yesterday for the first time and it was the first competitive sport I played in 15 years (the last time was high school gym class in freshman year !!), and I was actually good at it, and it filled me with joy but made me confront some kind of deep sadness: I avoided and missed out on sports all these years as a result of the controlling, oppressive environment I grew up in.

As a child I was good at sports but gave up quickly on sports as a teenager. Once I looked more "physically mature", my dogmatic, religious, and controlling mother shamed how "skin tight" and "revealing" athletic clothing was; I had to wear baggy clothes, shorts were banned, and I'd be a sweaty mess in 80 degree weather. I'd overheat and get in my head, and my high school bully would constantly point out how sweaty I was all the time. I started being terrible at sports because I'd just stay stuck in my head about how I just looked like a "sweaty loser" while all the other women my age looked free flowy in their athletic clothes and played freely. My posture was hunched, I had no confidence, I didn't look like anyone else, and I gave up on it all. "I'm not athletic, I'm just gross and sweaty and sluggy. I'll never be free like the other ppl". Why didn't I just secretly change to different clothing that was functional and confirming? Because it was deeply ingrained that there would be severe, aggressive, and abusive consequences at home if she found out.

Fast-forward years later, when I'm scared to try snowboarding at 26 but I do it anyway and I realize "oh, I CAN do sports". And now at 30, I clocked my fear and reluctance to participate in competitive sports bc I worried about being perceived as a "sluggy person that sucks at everything" - but then adult me traced it back all the way to childhood control and realized none of it exists now. And I had a freakin blast 😃 I can't believe I avoided competitive sports for 15 years but here we are now

i went to TEDx in Venice CA today and i rly liked the structure and format of a joint talk by Billy Baldwin @BillyBaldwin (politically left) and Dr. Robert Marbut @marbutr (politically right/conservative). in this talk they riffed off of each other to talk about addressing…

i went to TEDx in Venice CA today and i rly liked the structure and format of a joint talk by Billy Baldwin @BillyBaldwin (politically left) and Dr. Robert Marbut @marbutr (politically right/conservative). in this talk they riffed off of each other to talk about addressing homelessness and the fentanyl crisis in the US.
what they did well:
1) build trust with the audience and establish rapport with each other by introducing each other and, with humor, baldwin admits he wasn't even sure he was gonna like Marbut (real, relatable to prejudice, context-setting, address elephant in the room)
2) they then take turns presenting the actual problem (problem only, no solutions yet, facts) while kindly yielding time to each other - both clearly care about the issue and respect each other. establish emotional context and all-around empathy, audience buy-in
3) then they each offer a grounded critique the policies of their OWN political alignment where they acknowledge the intent but share where there was misfire - this registers as more credible to the audience bc it's not "side A bashes side B"
4) they then take turns sharing policy they both agree on

i wanna see more talks like this!!!

I'm not a teacher or coach or whatever, and I'm not "enlightened" (lol!!!) but I'll write things on here like diary-style reflections detailing growth arcs. I get public and private feedback from ppl about how it's helped them bc it's relatable, and it's affirming what feels like…

I'm not a teacher or coach or whatever, and I'm not "enlightened" (lol!!!) but I'll write things on here like diary-style reflections detailing growth arcs. I get public and private feedback from ppl about how it's helped them bc it's relatable, and it's affirming what feels like the best outcome of sharing. Bc if I wanted to write just to write (and I do)...I have a journal. 

And this brings me joy and purpose bc I love human connection. I've healed through stories and poetry by others, and also by writing my own. It feels like paying it forward via invitation to see me or see yourself in my stories and your stories; I am simply being myself and expressing myself and what I learned. I feel so free on here and I'm happy to share, connect w ppl, and support collective learning and community building.

I don't enjoy positioning myself like some kind of guru or "the wiser" bc we're all learning. I'm not a sage, I'm 30 and I have a lot of life that I still want to experience. It feels like walking alongside a lot of awesome ppl on here. I learn from ppl on here just as much as some learn from me; it's how the world should be, in my head. I'll learn wisdom from observing an earnest toddler as solidly as conversing with an 83-year-old wisewoman.

And then once in a while some dude will neg subtly and I know what's happening immediately (this is different than constructive critique which I am always, always open to. There's hallmark signs that it's a neg)

So what's happening?

"You can't be pretty AND intelligent AND badass!!!! What??? Not fair. You have to AT least be stupid. Come on. You're probably actually stupid. Women who look like you should be stupid or scared so there's some kind of nerf. Let me poke holes in what you're saying. Or maybe you'll cower or feel bad if I put you down? RAWRRrRR. Or maybe you're not that kind. Maybe you'll break. Provoke provoke. Take my bait"

Lol. Ok. 😂 ...thanks for the compliment? I must be doing something right! Dis a u thing. It's always a u thing sorry bro. You'll figure it out though. I believe in you

And no, I'm absolutely not perfect. But I know when noise is noise.

I finally found a deity statue that I liked specifically bc of the form and details in the carving. it's broken in one place but you can't even tell. I didn't resonate with the form of any of the brass Nataraja on display, and then after a lot of deliberation and almost getting one anyway, I saw this one in the backroom and instantly new the shopkeeper was like "oh that one isn't tagged. We weren't gonna sell it bc it's broken." So obviously...I buy it at a substantial discount :)

Nataraja symbolizes the cycles of creation and destruction in the universe so there's poetic irony in the statue being broken - gods are fallible and so am I, material embodiment is not forever but it's mine, and there's wholeness and divinity in even the broken. There's duality in gods being revered as divine and perfect but also broken, partial, and imperfect - this is the incomplete fractal of the eternal that i occupy too, in all my small corner of the universe and with my imperfections, for I cannot and will not be everything all at once although I am part of it. The bookshop person was like "are you sure you don't want to wait for another shipment?" and I was like "no he's perfect"

on balancing openness and friendliness with directness, boundaries, and unwanted intensity:

i like ppl and i have a legitimate affinity for platonic interactions of all sorts. at the same time i understand that it's genuinely sometimes not ppls' fault for getting confused by me…

on balancing openness and friendliness with directness, boundaries, and unwanted intensity:

i like ppl and i have a legitimate affinity for platonic interactions of all sorts. at the same time i understand that it's genuinely sometimes not ppls' fault for getting confused by me sometimes. i realize my 🤩 face is read as 😍 and my 😊 face is read as 🥰 pretty frequently. i appreciate it when ppl are attuned enough to understand when romantic attraction is unreciprocated (via small hints and redirects) and proceed to carry it in an appropriate container, and then we all keep vibing - i think a lot of ppl do this well! i respect this. bc i can feel when ppl might think or feel certain things but their intentions are the same as mine, which is simple friendliness and i love that.

for more persistent cases: sometimes i still continue to try to give indirect "i'm not interested like that hints" but after MANY "not interested" hints on my end i start to wonder, "is this confusion, or are you simply ignoring what i want in favor of what you want? or are you just trying to wear me down hoping i'll respond differently?" there's a difference between vibing with everyone in a relaxed, social way vs. following someone around and putting your persistent, focused attention on them. this situation eventually calls for more directness, and i'm working on handling this better/sooner - towards the end of vc, at some points i found myself "hiding away" from public areas because i "didn't want to be found" by a couple of persistent individuals even though i wanted to otherwise go out, find my friends, and be social with everyone

after a helpful convo with friends, i determined i would need to be more direct, and eventually was. i had one very public-facing moment at vc where i almost wanted to melt down afterwards from feeling secondhand embarrassment come off the other person

this isn't to shame anyone, but i think there's two-way responsibility here. yes, on me to be mindful of myself, but also i don't like feeling hardcore followed and the kind of experience i'm personally trying to have ignored by others. i had to fight over-indexing this feeling to "i don't want male attention at all" bc most men there felt attuned, respectful, and dope to be around! i'm still working through some things around managing attention but i had a good vc overall :)

trying to be solutions-oriented here, going forward: i liked that there was a consent practice during orientation. that set the tone pretty well. it focused on saying yes or no kindly when asked/invited to do something

in my case, i could have used more vc cultural affirmation…

trying to be solutions-oriented here, going forward: i liked that there was a consent practice during orientation. that set the tone pretty well. it focused on saying yes or no kindly when asked/invited to do something

in my case, i could have used more vc cultural affirmation on how to handle the case where you're not asked, but simply followed around. bc it's public space, and it almost feels weird to say "hey, i noticed you've been following me around and in a way where i feel this imbalance/pressure/persistent romantic intent and i'm not interested" or something - bc it's a public space! they're allowed to be there. AND other ppl are watching, and i'm aware of this. so for me, it's like this "how do i handle this?" despite me being friendly, it got mentally taxing to manage this after a while and i started hiding away before addressing the situation head-on and idek if i did it the best way. but i could see how addressing this in orientation might help ppl have appropriate behavior and help other women handle these situations in vc sooner, prevent uncomfortable vc experiences, and ultimately help with more women coming back to vc

i feel grateful to have the emotional and mental stability that i do to ultimately handle situations like this, alone and even in altered states of consciousness, but damn am i grateful to have friends before that to outlet some pure frustration and get perspective

i have a rly visual imagination and i love to draw + paint but AI def helps with speed of output and multi-frame storytelling! although it actually does still take time to tweak frames/facial expressions and chatGPT through content policy tantrums about the blood so i had to call it "red ceremonial paint". at some points i was tempted to do a manual paint-over. but still faster and cleaner than painting it all LOL

happy to share prompting tricks too. for this one, here was the general process:
1) generate the first/most emotional two-character frame and literally specify the inspo song to chatGPT and tell them to capture the mood
2) tweak character interactions and facial features (eg. "intensify their eye contact", "make him a whole 1.5 heads taller than her", get into descriptive tweaking of facial feature shapes as well)
3) then i have my characters looking how i envision and i generate the other frames, inputting it with the first frame where "the characters look right to me" - and chatGPT is gonna still wanna change their facial features subtly in other frames so you have to remind it with this "retain everything except ___" eg. ""retain everything except soften her brows/brow expression and have her look down"
4) i tend to work off of a frame specifying the emotional arc of each character and then soften specific features from there if it's too exaggerated or dramatic
5) after that i say "retain all but make the background ____"
6) i think if i wanted to be perfectionist here, i could actually ask it to generate a frame set with consistent backgrounds and lighting as well but i didn't exactly do this. still playing around with this as a tool!

you know how ppl say "if i say i'm going to do something, it makes me want to do it less because i already get the dopamine"? for me, it's like "if i say i'm going to do something, it makes me do it and follow through bc now i am accountable for a public statement" and i follow through. i actually operate on putting my money where my mouth is by writing it first and if it's public, it's actually getting done. i think it comes from an innate irritation and lack of trust when i notice performative behavior in others. this isn't positing that i'm better so much as presenting two, distinct behavioral patterns from what looks like the same initial action
